features.title: pi-context clone — import a substrate from another project as a content-addressed fork
features.description: A dual-surface command (library cloneSubstrate + Pi tool + CLI + orchestrator script) that copies a source substrate's content (config vocabulary + all blocks + relations.json + objects/) into a destination dir, mints a fresh substrate_id, records a substrate_derived_from_substrate edge to the source, and writes a provenance-bearing registry entry. Fork semantics preserve the one-id<->one-dir invariant. Per DEC-0002.
acceptance_criteria: cloneSubstrate copies config + all blocks + relations.json + objects/ into the destination dir
the destination receives a freshly minted substrate_id distinct from the source
a substrate_derived_from_substrate edge records destination-derived-from-source lineage
the destination registry entry carries origin/provenance (derived-from source id + imported_at)
no substrate_id is duplicated across dirs (one-id<->one-dir SoT invariant holds)
surfaced as pi-context clone <source> <dest-dir> across library + Pi tool + CLI + orchestrator script
context-validate is clean on the cloned substrate

features.title: pi-context substrate merge (+ cherry-pick) — structure-aware 3-way reconciliation of two divergent substrate versions
features.description: A defined merge operation over items, edges, config, and objects, keyed off the content-parent DAG: computes the merge base from lineage, 3-way-merges per item with field-kind dispatch (sequence fields via FEAT-003), set-merges edges, unions the object store, 3-way-merges config/schema, records multi-parent lineage, re-validates all invariants, and emits a typed conflict set. Cherry-pick is a scoped sub-case. Per DEC-0004.
acceptance_criteria: For base B and descendants X, Y the driver produces one merged state taking all non-conflicting changes from both, with a typed conflict set for the rest, and identical output regardless of which side is named 'ours'
The merge base is computed from the content_parent DAG, not supplied externally
Edges merge by set semantics; the object store merges by union; sequence fields merge via the convergent sequence field-type (FEAT-003)
The merged state passes the full invariant + lens-validator pass, or every failure appears in the conflict set
Merged items record content_parent referencing both source versions (multi-parent)
A single-item delta from one branch applies onto another (cherry-pick) through the same machinery

features.title: Convergent ordered-sequence field-kind — CRDT-backed ordered item-refs that merge deterministically under concurrent insert/delete/move
features.description: A declared sequence-field kind over item-refs: a non-interleaving Fugue-class sequence CRDT (dense, immutable per-element position identifiers) plus a Kleppmann move-CRDT position register resolved by hybrid logical clock, with a sort-on-read projection to a plain ordered ref-array and array-position hand-edits translated to CRDT ops. CRDT state lives in the owning <block>.json; projection + field-aware merge live in block-api. One field-kind plugin into the substrate merge driver (FEAT-002). Per DEC-0005.
acceptance_criteria: Two divergent versions each insert, delete, and/or move elements in the same collection; the merge yields one replica-independent sequence containing all inserts, with no element duplicated or lost and no interleaving of concurrently-inserted runs
Insert mints one position identifier and mutates no neighbor
Move rewrites one element's position register; concurrent moves of the same element resolve deterministically by hybrid logical clock
The sort-on-read projection reproduces single-writer authoring order identically to a plain array, and accepts array-position hand-edits translated to CRDT operations
